channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As the risk of measles grows, why are parents so divided on vaccines?
item.description: In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.
item.content:encoded:
In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.

(Image credit: Mike Belleme for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
item.description: One of Bolsonaro's doctor's described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."
item.content:encoded:
One of Bolsonaro's doctor's described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

(Image credit: Eraldo Peres)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Opinion: An ancient, sophisticated palate
item.description: Researchers looking at foodcrusts on the pottery shards of ancient humans say there's evidence of a wide variety of ingredients, indicating that they may have been experimenting with "recipes."
item.content:encoded:
Researchers looking at foodcrusts on the pottery shards of ancient humans say there's evidence of a wide variety of ingredients, indicating that they may have been experimenting with "recipes."

(Image credit: Martin Bernetti)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Why women have an especially tough time in Senegal's prisons
item.description: Women charged with a crime in Senegal are at the mercy of a slow judicial process and prisons that may lack basic supplies. They also face stigma that robs them of familial and community support.
item.content:encoded:
Women charged with a crime in Senegal are at the mercy of a slow judicial process and prisons that may lack basic supplies. They also face stigma that robs them of familial and community support.

(Image credit: Ricci Shryock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ricci Shryock

item.title: With boom in prediction markets, some lawmakers worry about how to police themselves
item.description: House and Senate ethics committees give no financial disclosure guidance on event contracts or prediction markets — unlike stock, cryptocurrency and bond trades.
item.content:encoded:
House and Senate ethics committees give no financial disclosure guidance on event contracts or prediction markets — unlike stock, cryptocurrency and bond trades.

(Image credit: Luke Garrett for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: U.S. military bombs Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub, Trump says
item.description: President Trump said on Friday the U.S. military had "totally obliterated" military targets in Kharg Island, home to the primary terminal that handles Iran's oil exports. This as all six crew members on a refueling plane that went down in western Iraq were confirmed dead.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said on Friday the U.S. military had "totally obliterated" military targets in Kharg Island, home to the primary terminal that handles Iran's oil exports. This as all six crew members on a refueling plane that went down in western Iraq were confirmed dead.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Reframing Georgia O'Keeffe's legacy and protecting the land she loved
item.description: Georgia O'Keeffe called the New Mexico high desert "my country," but Pueblo peoples predated her. A more complex view is emerging amid efforts to preserve the land.
item.content:encoded:
Georgia O'Keeffe called the New Mexico high desert "my country," but Pueblo peoples predated her. A more complex view is emerging amid efforts to preserve the land.

(Image credit: Minesh Bacrania for NPR)
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: House GOP leadership silent as more members post anti-Muslim statements
item.description: A growing number of Republicans in Congress are embracing rhetoric against Muslims. Their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.
item.content:encoded:
A growing number of Republicans in Congress are embracing rhetoric against Muslims. Their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Helpful or harmful? How to vet tax advice from social media
item.description: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.content:encoded: So you heard a piece of tax advice from a friend or on social media that sounds interesting. Should you try it? A certified public accountant explains how to vet the claim — and avoid getting scammed.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: These are the casualties and cost of the war in Iran 2 weeks into the conflict
item.description: The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
